message:
fix(process): close PATH-walk vs setenv UAF in execvp_via_execve

REVIEW round 4 (same defense class as 7c5b8200/d088570b):
process_abi::path_bytes_from_env_vector walked the libc-global
`environ` array reading entries via CStr::from_ptr without holding
ENVIRON_LOCK. A concurrent setenv from another thread that triggered
host_passthrough_realloc on the environ array would free the memory
under our cursor — UAF.

Reachability: every multi-threaded program that calls execvp / execlp
on a path that requires PATH search (no '/' in the file argument)
runs this walk before the execve. Threads still active at execvp time
can race the env walk.

Fix: expose ENVIRON_LOCK and a helper `with_environ_locked` from
stdlib_abi, then have path_bytes_from_env_vector route the
process-global walk through it. Caller-supplied envp pointers are
walked directly (the caller owns that array's lifetime, not libc).

Full abi suite: 2976/2976 pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
